summary: First-pass full-stack testing of Kids Feast catering MVP. Backend: 19/20 pytest tests passed (one brute-force lockout test failed due to ingress IP variability across pods). Frontend: All public pages load and render seeded content correctly; 6-step quote calculator works with dynamic estimate; admin login, protected route redirect, all 7 admin tabs, package CRUD path, and logout all working.

critical.issue: Lockout fails in this environment because the rate-limit identifier uses `request.client.host` which is the immediate ingress/proxy IP. Behind a load balancer (Kubernetes ingress here) requests for the same user arrive from MULTIPLE source IPs (observed 10.208.130.77 and 10.208.130.78 splitting attempts 4+2), so the per-identifier counter never reaches the 5-attempt threshold. This effectively defeats the brute-force protection in production.
critical.fix_suggestion: Use `request.headers.get('X-Forwarded-For', '').split(',')[0].strip() or request.client.host` for ip, or key the rate limit solely on the lowercased email (more deterministic) instead of ip:email.

minor.issue: backend/.env has `CORS_ORIGINS="*"` while server uses `allow_credentials=True`. The wildcard is still injected into the allowed origins list. Browsers reject `Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *` with credentials. It happens to work today only because FRONTEND_URL is appended explicitly and CORSMiddleware picks the first matching exact origin, but the `*` entry is misleading/dead config and should be removed or replaced with explicit origins.

Requires authentication (Depends(get_current_user)). If the access token is missing/expired, logout returns 401 and frontend silently swallows it. Cookies might remain set on the client. Consider making logout idempotent (always clear cookies).

design_issues.screen: Quote step 6 - Your Details / Step 1 Preferred date
issues: Uses native browser date picker (<input type=date>) instead of a shadcn calendar component as recommended for consistent styling across browsers.
Phone input uses placeholder '+1 555 555 5555' but type is text (not tel). Adds no mobile keypad on phones and made automated tel-locator targeting fail. Recommend type='tel'.
First tab label is 'Quote Requests' while the stat card / spec calls it 'Quotes'. Minor naming inconsistency.

action_items: Fix brute-force lockout identifier so it survives multi-IP ingress (use X-Forwarded-For or email-only key).
Tighten CORS: remove `*` from CORS_ORIGINS in backend/.env since credentials=True is enabled; rely on FRONTEND_URL.
Make /api/auth/logout idempotent (don't require auth, always clear cookies).
Use type='tel' for phone input on /quote step 6; consider shadcn Calendar/DatePicker for preferred date.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is ~545 lines and mixes models, auth, routes, seeding. Consider splitting into models/, routes/, auth/, seed/ modules for maintainability.
PATCH /api/admin/quotes/{id} accepts a raw dict for status — should be a typed Pydantic model with allowed status enum to prevent invalid status writes.

_make_admin_crud accepts a raw dict and forwards to model_cls(**body); this silently ignores missing required fields only if the model has defaults. Update endpoint stores arbitrary keys (no validation) — a malicious admin could pollute documents. Validate updates against the Pydantic model too.
Quote estimated_total is taken from the request body (client-controlled). Backend should recompute it from package + guest_count + snack box to prevent tampering.
Login does not normalise email for the rate-limit key when the user does not exist (it is normalised — OK), but successful login response uses user['email'] casing from DB. Minor consistency note.
JWT exp uses 60 min access + 7d refresh, but frontend does not call /auth/refresh anywhere — long sessions will silently 401 after an hour with no auto-refresh on the client.
seed_data_creation: No new seed data. Test data was created (TEST_Package, TEST quote, brute_<ts>@example.com login attempts) and cleaned up via mongosh.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend tests live at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py; run with `pytest /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py -v --junitxml=/app/test_reports/pytest/pytest_results.xml`. The brute-force lockout test is environment-sensitive: in this kubernetes preview the ingress source IP varies across requests, so the per-(ip:email) counter never accumulates to 5. After any code change that switches the identifier to X-Forwarded-For or email-only, clear db.login_attempts before re-running: `mongosh kidsfeast_db --eval 'db.login_attempts.deleteMany({})'`. All public endpoints, auth, admin CRUD, and quote submission were verified end-to-end via both pytest and Playwright. Admin tab label is 'Quote Requests' (not 'Quotes').
rca_of_the_issue: Brute-force lockout RCA: In server.py login() the identifier is `f"{ip}:{email}"` where ip = request.client.host. In Kubernetes with multi-replica ingress, request.client.host is the per-hop proxy IP, which differs across requests for the same client. Verified by inspecting db.login_attempts after sending 6 wrong logins for the same email — entries split across 10.208.130.77 (count=4) and 10.208.130.78 (count=2). Neither reached the 5-attempt lock threshold. Mitigation: extract client IP from `X-Forwarded-For` header (first value) before falling back to request.client.host, OR key the throttle on email only.
